summary.intent: Fix newly launched browser sessions defaulting to host mode instead of sandboxed mode when created from the UI.
summary.outcome: Added a `default_sandbox` flag to `RealBrowserService` that injects `sandbox: true` when the field is absent, wired to the sandbox config mode in server.rs. Changes compiled, passed clippy, and were committed and pushed.

repo: `request.sandbox.unwrap_or(false)` in services.rs causes all UI-initiated sessions to run in host mode since the UI never sends `sandbox: true`.
Sandbox config lives at `config.tools.exec.sandbox.mode` in crates/config/src/schema.rs; mode values include at least 'none' and 'all'.
The sandbox router/config is constructed later in the startup sequence than the browser service, making direct dependency injection awkward.

code.finding: `request.sandbox.unwrap_or(false)` — sandbox defaults to false (host mode) when not explicitly set by the caller.
Sandbox config is built after the browser service is initialized, so `default_sandbox` is wired by checking `config.tools.exec.sandbox.mode != 'none'` directly rather than via the sandbox router.

workflow: When initialization order prevents clean dependency injection, reading config directly at the call site is a pragmatic alternative to restructuring.
Grepping for trait definitions and config struct fields was the fastest way to understand the sandbox architecture without reading all files.

friction: Sandbox router is constructed after the browser service in server.rs, requiring a workaround (direct config inspection) instead of clean dependency injection.
The sandbox mode enum/string values were not immediately obvious and required multiple grep passes to confirm valid values.
open_items: The initialization order issue (browser service constructed before sandbox router) is a structural debt — a cleaner fix would restructure startup order or pass sandbox config earlier.
The UI never sends `sandbox: true` — it may be worth auditing whether the UI should explicitly control sandbox mode rather than relying on a server-side default.
